name: Perplexity Computer
description: Perplexity's computer-use agents autonomously browse the web, extract structured data, fill forms, and complete multi-step research workflows. Leverages Perplexity's web indexing and citation-grounded search for agents that navigate live websites and return structured outputs with source attribution.
oneLiner: AI-powered computer use and web data extraction agents with citation-grounded research capabilities.
pricing: Free (basic search); Pro $10/mo (unlimited Pro Search, 15 Deep Searches/mo); Max $200/mo (Computer, Personal Computer, unlimited everything)
capabilities: Computer — orchestrates 19 AI models in parallel for complex multi-step tasks
Personal Computer — dedicated local device with persistent file/app access
Tax return preparation from uploaded financial documents
Multi-model orchestration matching tasks to optimal models

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: NL prompts or API calls define research tasks. Agent browses, extracts, compiles results. Prompt-first with minimal config.
strengths: Massive user base for feedback loops
notableFeatures: Computer-use agents for live website interaction
Hybrid search combining index + real-time browsing

recentDevelopments: 2026-02-25: Computer launched — multi-model AI agent orchestrating 19 models for complex tasks
2026-03-11: Personal Computer launched — local Mac Mini with persistent access to files and apps
2026-03: Revenue jumped 50%, ARR tops $450M — driven by agent capabilities
2026: Tax preparation agent — drafts federal returns from uploaded financial documents
2026: $200/month Perplexity Max tier for Computer access

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: NL prompting is intuitive. API for programmatic creation.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Citation trails provide visibility. Dedicated tools still early.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Solid API docs. Computer-use docs newer.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Prompt-based iteration is fast. No build steps.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Shared API keys. No version control.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Web search as context loader. No enterprise RAG.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Query and citation logs. No SOC2 confirmed for computer-use.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Search infra handles high volumes. Computer-use more resource-intensive.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Basic usage dashboards. No advanced observability.

scoringRationale: 59/100 overall. Strongest: Creation (15/20). Weakest: Orchestration (8/20). Breakdown: Creation (15/20), Orchestration (8/20), Integration (12/20), Governance (10/20), Operations (14/20).
targetUser: Teams needing citation-grounded web research at scale

walkthrough.title: Perplexity: PM & CTO Walkthrough — AI Research Platform

steps.title: Profile Instructions
steps.description: Custom AI behavior: writing style, tone, formatting. Structured guidelines for consistent responses.

works: Profile customization makes every search personalized
Structured writing guidelines ensure consistent output style
doesntWork: Profile applies globally — no per-search customization
No profile sharing across team members
pmCritique.userPsychology: Profile instructions are Perplexity's version of system prompts — but for search, not agents.

ctoCritique.verdict: Profile customization is useful for individual researchers but not manageable at enterprise scale. No team profiles, no enforcement.

steps.title: Pro Plan — $10/month
steps.description: Pro: $10/mo (save $48/yr annual). Unlimited Pro Search, 15 Deep Searches/month, Unlimited paper Snapshots.

works: $10/mo is accessible — lower barrier than enterprise platforms
Unlimited Pro Search removes usage anxiety
Deep Search limited to 15/mo creates scarcity for premium feature
doesntWork: 15 Deep Searches/mo is very limiting for power users
No enterprise tier visible in this view
Paper Snapshots is academic-focused — niche feature
pmCritique.userPsychology: $10/mo makes Perplexity an impulse purchase for knowledge workers. The limit on Deep Search creates natural upgrade pressure.

ctoCritique.verdict: $10/mo per seat is cheap for research. But 15 Deep Searches/mo per user at 100 seats = 1500/mo total, which may not be enough for research-heavy teams.
concerns: Per-user pricing doesn't scale for large teams
Deep Search limits may block research workflows
No volume or enterprise pricing visible
enterpriseGaps: No usage analytics for team management

steps.title: Search with Citations
steps.description: Paris 2024 Olympics medal table with sourced data. Clear formatting with country rankings, medal counts, and historical analysis.

works: Every claim has a source — citation-backed search builds trust
Structured data presentation (tables, charts) is clean and professional
Follow-up questions enable research drilling without re-prompting
doesntWork: Citations link to sources but verification is manual
Data may be outdated — no freshness indicator per fact
No export to spreadsheet or structured format
pmCritique.userPsychology: Citation-backed search is Perplexity's moat. In a world of hallucinating AI, showing sources is the trust differentiator.

ctoCritique.verdict: Citations are valuable for agent grounding — agents that cite sources are more trustworthy. But citation accuracy needs verification infrastructure.
concerns: Citation accuracy not guaranteed — sources may not support the claim
enterpriseGaps: No citation verification API for automated fact-checking
No citation export for compliance documentation

steps.title: Spaces for Research
steps.description: Organized research collections with shared access and file uploads. Collaborative research workspace.

works: Spaces organize research by topic — natural knowledge management
File uploads enable private data integration
doesntWork: Spaces UX not visible in detail — only mentioned conceptually
File upload limits and formats unclear
pmCritique.userPsychology: Spaces transform Perplexity from a search tool to a research platform. The ability to organize findings by project changes the workflow.

ctoCritique.verdict: Spaces could serve as agent knowledge bases — organized, shareable research contexts. API access to Spaces would be valuable for agent integration.

steps.title: Deep Research Mode
steps.description: Multi-step research: planning, searching across sources, synthesis. Comprehensive analysis for complex questions.

works: Deep Research automates multi-step research workflows
Planning → searching → synthesis mirrors how human researchers work
Comprehensive output covers multiple angles of a topic
doesntWork: 15/month limit is restrictive for regular use
Research depth vs speed trade-off not controllable
No custom research templates or methodologies
pmCritique.userPsychology: Deep Research is Perplexity's most valuable feature for enterprise. Automated multi-source research in minutes instead of hours.

ctoCritique.verdict: Deep Research as an API endpoint would be extremely valuable as an agent tool — any agent could trigger comprehensive research as a step in its workflow.
concerns: API access to Deep Research unclear
No partial results or streaming during long research

steps.title: API Access
steps.description: Developer API for programmatic search. Integration point for agents and automated workflows.

works: API enables programmatic access — agents can use Perplexity as a tool
Structured output format for machine consumption
doesntWork: API capabilities vs web UI capabilities unknown — may be limited
Rate limits and pricing not shown in this view
No agent-specific SDK or integration pattern
pmCritique.userPsychology: The API is what makes Perplexity relevant to the agent factory market — not as a platform, but as a tool that every agent should have access to.

ctoCritique.verdict: Perplexity API as an agent tool is a strong integration. Need to evaluate: rate limits, cost per query, latency, and output format compatibility with our agent framework.
concerns: API rate limits and pricing critical for agent integration
Output format compatibility with agent frameworks

pmVerdict.summary: Perplexity is the best AI-powered research tool, not an agent factory. Profile customization, Pro Search with citations, Spaces for organized research, and Deep Research for multi-step analysis are excellent. But it's a search/research product, not an agent builder — you can't create custom agents or automate workflows. Relevant as a research tool that agents should integrate with, not as a platform to build agents on.
strengths: Best citation-backed AI search — every answer has sources
Deep Research mode for comprehensive multi-step analysis
risks: Not an agent factory — can't build custom agents
No workflow automation or agent orchestration

ctoVerdict.summary: Perplexity is a research tool our agents should use, not a platform to build agents on. The API enables programmatic search with citations, which is valuable as a tool in our agent toolkit. Evaluate as an integration target, not a competitor to agent platforms.
ctoVerdict.decision: INTEGRATE — Use as Agent Tool, Not Platform
strengths: Citation-backed search API for agent grounding
Pro Search provides depth beyond standard web search
Spaces API could enable agent-specific research contexts
dealBreakers: Not an agent platform — can't build or deploy agents
Consumer pricing model, not enterprise licensing
API rate limits may constrain agent usage at scale

youtubeReviews.title: Learn 80% of Perplexity in under 10 minutes!

coreCompetence.bestAt: AI-powered web research agent backed by Perplexity search — combines real-time web search with computer-use for research-intensive tasks.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Knowledge workers and researchers who want AI to handle complex web research, data gathering, and synthesis tasks.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins on search quality — Perplexity search engine is best-in-class for factual web queries. Wins on research use case. Loses on general agent capabilities. Cloud-only, limited enterprise features.
